steps.description: Heat the (0001) sapphire substrate with a 2-degree off-cut in ultra-high vacuum to 700°C.

Grow MoSe2 at 300°C with a very small growth rate of about 1 monolayer (0.7 nm) per hour. Selenium is deposited from a standard low-temperature effusion cell and molybdenum is deposited using an e-beam with a rod (1/4 inch diameter, 35 mm length).
Every 20 h (14 nm of MoSe2), the sample is annealed at 700°C for 1 h to avoid vertical growth and keep the surface flat.
After growth, the samples are manually polished using silk in the environment of ethylene glycol to improve surface quality and remove nanopillars.

synthesis.notes: Total thickness of MoSe2 layer is 42 nm. Growth rate is ~1 monolayer (0.7 nm) per hour. Annealing is performed every 20 h (every 14 nm) at 700°C for 1 h to suppress vertical growth. After growth, mechanical polishing with silk in ethylene glycol reduces surface roughness from Sq=14.6 nm to Sq=2.0 nm. Large (few cm2) homogeneous layers are produced.
